sorting.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort in the worst case?
sorting.explanation: Bubble Sort compares adjacent elements and swaps them. In the worst case it needs n*(n-1)/2 comparisons, giving O(n²).

sorting.question: Which sorting algorithm is most efficient for nearly sorted data?
sorting.explanation: Insertion Sort performs at O(n) for nearly sorted arrays as very few swaps are needed.

sorting.question: What is the space complexity of Merge Sort?
sorting.explanation: Merge Sort requires O(n) extra space for the temporary arrays used during merging.

sorting.question: Which sorting algorithm uses the divide and conquer strategy?
sorting.explanation: Quick Sort divides the array around a pivot element and recursively sorts sub-arrays.

sorting.question: What is the best case time complexity of Quick Sort?
sorting.explanation: In the best case, Quick Sort's pivot always divides the array into two equal halves giving O(n log n).

cpu-scheduling.question: Which CPU scheduling algorithm can cause starvation?
cpu-scheduling.explanation: Priority Scheduling can starve low-priority processes indefinitely if high-priority processes keep arriving.

cpu-scheduling.question: What is the time quantum in Round Robin scheduling?
options: A fixed time slice given to each process
cpu-scheduling.explanation: Round Robin assigns a fixed time quantum to each process in circular order.

cpu-scheduling.question: FCFS scheduling is:
cpu-scheduling.explanation: FCFS (First Come First Served) is non-preemptive. Once a process gets the CPU it runs until completion.

cpu-scheduling.question: Which scheduling algorithm minimizes average waiting time for a given set of processes?
cpu-scheduling.explanation: SRTF (Shortest Remaining Time First) is optimal for minimizing average waiting time.

cpu-scheduling.question: Turnaround time is defined as:
cpu-scheduling.explanation: Turnaround time = Completion time - Arrival time. It includes both waiting time and burst time.

page-replacement.question: Which page replacement algorithm causes Belady's anomaly?
page-replacement.explanation: Belady's anomaly occurs in FIFO where increasing frames can sometimes increase page faults.

page-replacement.question: The Optimal page replacement algorithm replaces:
options: The page not used for longest time in future
page-replacement.explanation: Optimal algorithm replaces the page that will not be used for the longest time in the future.

page-replacement.question: LRU stands for:
page-replacement.explanation: LRU (Least Recently Used) replaces the page that has not been used for the longest time in the past.

page-replacement.question: A page fault occurs when:
options: A page is found in memory
A requested page is not in main memory
A page is written to disk
page-replacement.explanation: A page fault is triggered when the CPU accesses a page that is not currently in main memory.

page-replacement.question: Thrashing in virtual memory refers to:
options: Excessive page faults causing low CPU utilization
page-replacement.explanation: Thrashing occurs when processes spend more time swapping pages than executing, causing very low CPU utilization.

disk-scheduling.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm services the request closest to the current head position?
disk-scheduling.explanation: SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First) always services the request nearest to the current head position.

disk-scheduling.question: The SCAN algorithm is also known as:
disk-scheduling.explanation: SCAN moves the disk arm like an elevator, servicing requests in one direction then reversing.

disk-scheduling.question: C-SCAN differs from SCAN in that:
options: It reverses direction at the end
It jumps back to the beginning and scans in one direction only
disk-scheduling.explanation: C-SCAN provides more uniform wait times by returning to the beginning and scanning only in one direction.

disk-scheduling.question: Total seek time is minimized best by:
disk-scheduling.explanation: SSTF minimizes individual seek times by always picking the nearest request, though it can cause starvation.

disk-scheduling.question: FCFS disk scheduling is:
options: Fair but may cause high seek times
disk-scheduling.explanation: FCFS is fair (no starvation) but can result in high total seek times due to random order of requests.
